title: Climate scientists aren't surprised by summer's record heat: 'We've entered a dangerous zone'
subhead: Record heat, historic drought and sweeping wildfires have marked France's 2026 summer. Yet climate scientists mostly see global warming's expected effects playing out. Only the rapid succession of heatwaves has caught them off guard.

title: France set to ban social media for under-15s, a first in the EU
Iran bets on war of attrition against the US
Burnham enters Downing Street and appoints former defense minister John Healey as chancellor of the Exchequer
subhead: The former mayor of Greater Manchester, who succeeds Keir Starmer, has become the seventh British prime minister in 10 years. Ed Miliband, who had aspired to the chancellorship, was named foreign secretary.

title: Two months before Russian parliamentary elections, antiwar voices still struggle for traction
subhead: The Kremlin continues to stifle the few parliamentary candidates opposed to the war in Ukraine, even as the public and some industrialists begin to imagine peace.

title: Between China and the US, a new diplomacy of detainees emerges
subhead: Alongside the major issues discussed between the world's two leading powers, the fate of American citizens imprisoned in China on espionage charges has also become a bargaining chip.

title: Bernard Arnault, a 'free-marketeer' who whispers in the ear of every president
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (2/6). LVMH's boss has never hidden his allegiance to France's pro-business, establishment right. He's close to fellow billionaire Vincent Bolloré and, above all, pragmatic. Now he's preparing for the far right's rise to power.

Didier Deschamps bids farewell to French team after spectacular World Cup defeat
subhead: The coach stepped down after a dramatic 6-4 loss in the World Cup third-place match against England on Saturday. Over his 14 years in the role, he nonetheless managed to restore France to the pinnacle of world football.

title: French 'libertines' rapes case: From livestreams to camp sites, where the sexual crimes took place
subhead: Christophe B. and many other men he recruited are accused of raping women, with these sexual assaults broadcast online and in public. Responsibility is now being called into question. This is the final part of our investigation into the French 'libertines' rapes case.
byline: Jérôme Lefilliâtre,  Lorraine de Foucher  and  Morgane Tual

title: Inside the court of France's richest man, Bernard Arnault
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (1/6). The billionaire at the head of LVMH is as secretive as he is powerful. He relies on a devoted entourage in which a mix of fear and admiration prevails.
